leadText: For the Japanese merchandise collection named Hyper Beam, see Hyper Beam collection. Hyper Beam (Japanese: はかいこうせん Destruction Beam) is a damage-dealing Normal-type move introduced in Generation I. It is: TM15 from Generation I to Pokémon Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon, and in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl. TM48 in Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee!. TM08 in Pokémon Sword and Shield. TM163 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet. TM044 in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation I 1.2 Generation II 1.3 Generation III 1.4 Generation IV onwards 1.5 Generation VIII 1.5.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.6 Generation IX 1.6.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation I [ edit | edit source ] Hyper Beam inflicts damage. A recharge turn is required on the turn after damage is done, during which no action may be performed. Hyper Beam will not require a recharge turn if it misses, it breaks a substitute , it knocks out the target, the user is targeted by a binding move (even if it misses), the user flinches , or the user is put to sleep after the attack but before the recharge turn. However, being frozen does not prevent the need for the recharge turn of Hyper Beam. If the user is confused , the recharge turn of Hyper Beam will not count towards the number of turns the user will remain confused. If one Pokémon attacks first with a binding move that misses in a round in which the other Pokémon must recharge from a use of Hyper Beam, the user of Hyper Beam will automatically use Hyper Beam again during that turn instead of recharging. If at such a time Hyper Beam has 0 PP , Hyper Beam will still be used, and afterward its PP will roll over to 63 , and full PP Ups will be applied to it. An opponent can still use items on the user while it's recharging, in which case it will recharge the turn after. In Pokémon Stadium , Hyper Beam requires a recharge turn every time it is used. Generation II [ edit | edit source ] Hyper Beam will always need to recharge if it hits, regardless if it knocks the opponent or a substitute out, unless the battle is completed in that turn or the attack misses. The binding move glitch has been fixed. Generation III [ edit | edit source ] If the user has Truant , the recharge and "loafing around" take place on the same turn. Hyper Beam is now affected by the King's Rock . Generation IV onwards [ edit | edit source ] Hyper Beam is now special instead of physical, with a physical equivalent, Giga Impact added. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Legends: Arceus , Hyper Beam reduces the user's action speed instead of forcing it to recharge. Pokémon Legends: Arceus Category Special PP 5 Agile Strong Power 120 100 150 Accuracy 90% 90% 95% Action speed modifier Self +5 +2 +8 Target 0 +3 0 Additional effect ? ? ? ? ? ? None Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Special Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power 150 180 Cooldown 20s Move wind-up time 35 frames Effect duration —s Additional effect — — — None

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Stad Stad2 An extremely powerful attack. The attacker becomes so tired, it has to rest the next turn. G S C 1st turn: Attack 2nd turn: Rest R S E Colo XD Powerful, but leaves the user immobile the next turn. (move) Powerful, but needs recharging the next turn. (TM) FR LG A severely damaging attack that makes the user rest on the next turn. D P Pt HG SS PBR The foe is attacked with a powerful beam. The user must rest on the next turn to regain its energy. B W B2 W2 The target is attacked with a powerful beam. The user must rest on the next turn to regain its energy. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP S V The target is attacked with a powerful beam. The user can't move on the next turn. LA The target is attacked with a powerful beam. ZA The user attacks by shooting a powerful beam at targets. The recoil will leave the user unable to move for a short time.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Inflicts damage on the target, even at a distance. It also inflicts the Paused status on the user, making it incapable of action. MD T D S Inflicts damage on the target, even at a distance, but it also afflicts the user with a Paused status condition. A Pokémon with the Paused status is incapable of action. B S L はなれたポケモンに ダメージをあたえる しかし じぶんが ようすみじょうたいに なってしまう ようすみじょうたいの ポケモンは こうどうできない MD GtI It damages even a faraway Pokémon. But it also causes the Recoil status condition, which prevents you from doing anything on the next turn. S MD You'll explode, causing damage to a Pokémon, even one far away. The explosion affects the surroundings. Walls and items on the ground will also disappear. But the recoil prevents you from doing anything on the next turn. MD RT DX It damages Pokémon, even those far away, and causes an explosion that also affects the surroundings. Walls and items on the ground will also disappear. But after you use the move, the Recoil prevents you from doing anything on the next turn. It damages faraway Pokémon. But it also gives you the Recoil status, which prevents you from doing anything on the next turn. *

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] The Pokéquiz formatting error Most Pokémon that can learn Giga Impact through TM can learn Hyper Beam through TM , and vice versa. It and Giga Impact can be learned via TM by many fully evolved Pokémon that can learn TMs, including Pokémon that were fully evolved at one point but later received one or more evolved forms, such as Porygon or Seadra . In Generation I , the Dratini family were the only Pokémon not fully evolved or single stage that could learn Hyper Beam, though possibly due to an oversight could only get it by level up not TM. On Kids' WB! , a Pokémon: Orange Island Adventures Pokéquiz segment aired during Pokémon Food Fight! incorrectly formatted Hyper Beam's name as " Hyperbeam ." In the Brazilian dub of the animated series , Hyper Beam has been given many names, such as Hiper-farol , Hiper-fusão , Super-chama , and Raio Solar , the last one tending to cause much confusion because it is the Brazilian dub name of Solar Beam . In the Dutch dub of the animated series, especially in the first few seasons, it's often incorrectly translated to "Hydro Straal", which translates to "Hydro Beam". In the Generation I games and Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen , there is a quiz question in Cinnabar Gym that asks if TM28 contains Tombstoner R B Y or Tombstony FR LG . In the original Japanese versions, the move is called しねしねこうせん Shineshine Kōsen , which can translate into a silly expression such as "Die-Die Beam" or "Die! Beam", playing on Hyper Beam's Japanese name, はかいこうせん Hakai Kōsen (Destruction Beam). Since Generation VI , the move has the same sound effect as it originally did in Generations I and II . In Pokémon Masters EX , Hyper Beam was the first usable move to cost four move-gauge slots. It premiered on Lance & Dragonite on December 26, 2019.
